answer:
The Firecrawl MCP server is an official integration that allows LLM clients like Claude Desktop and Cursor to access Firecrawl's web scraping, search, and autonomous research capabilities via the Model Context Protocol [1][2]. It converts dynamic website content into LLM-ready markdown or structured JSON [2].

You can run the server locally using npx or connect via the hosted endpoint [2][3]. Authentication uses your Firecrawl API key or an OAuth bearer token [1][3].

To run locally:
```bash
env FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-YOUR_API_KEY npx -y firecrawl-mcp
```

Key features include single-page scraping, site crawling, web search, structured data extraction, and an autonomous research agent [4][5][6]. While some basic features work on a rate-limited, keyless tier, advanced tools like crawling and extraction require an API key [3]. Configuration involves adding the server command to your MCP client settings file [2].
